bot_answer: Search for the book in Primo, click “Place Hold,” and sign in [1]. Then choose Oxford Campus / King Library as the pickup location, and wait for the notification that it is ready for pickup [1][3].
The loan period for graduate students is listed on the Miami University Libraries Circulation Policies page (see the “Loan periods” information for graduate students) [1].

I can't renew books or check renewal status directly. Please renew online at https://ohiolink-mu.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/account?vid=01OHIOLINK_MU:MU&section=overview&lang=en or contact a librarian for help.

The liaisons page lists Roger Justus with the email justusra@miamioh.edu [1].
I can't submit ILL requests directly, but I can show you how to request items from other libraries.

Yes. Adobe Creative Cloud access through the library includes Premiere Pro among the available products, and the software checkout page lists Adobe Creative Cloud for students to download and use on their own devices [3][4][5].
King Library is closed today, so the MakerSpace is not open right now [1].

The University Archives contact is Archives@MiamiOH.edu, and the general Special Collections inbox is SpecColl@MiamiOH.edu [1][2]. Special Collections & University Archives is on the 3rd Floor of King Library in Oxford [1][2].
Walter Havighurst Special Collections & University Archives is Closed today (Saturday, 2026-05-23) [1]. The hours page also shows it Closed for every day in the week of 2026-05-23 [1].

Yes — Oxford Library data services offers consultations and GIS help, and library staff are happy to help with GIS projects [1][2][4]. You can also use the GIS resources article for examples of tools and support available through the libraries [2][5].
Use the Printing & WiFi page for Oxford printing policy information [1][3]. It is the canonical library page for current printing details [3][4].

Use the Ask Us page to email your question to the library; it lists a “By Email” option for general questions [1][2]. You can also use the Contact Us page, which includes a “Contact us through email” option [4].

The Oxford sources point to the library food/drink policy page for the current rules on bringing food into the library [1][2][3]. One Oxford library event announcement also shows food can be present in King Library during a special program, but it does not state the general policy [4].

Yes. The software on library computers page says additional titles such as MATLAB can be added to library computers using Software Center on campus Windows PCs and Self-Service on campus Macs [1][4][5]. For the current software page, see https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/use/technology/software/ [3].

See the software-on-library-computers page for the current list of software available on library computers [4]. It notes that the page covers software on library computers and that the link itself is sufficient for this question [4].

The sources provided only show the liaisons directory, which lists Abigail Morgan and Roger Justus [1][2]. Use the directory page to identify the current contact for circulation-related help: https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/about/organization/liaisons/ [1][2].
Same-day for in-stock items; it can take longer if the book comes from storage or another campus, and you’ll get an email when it’s ready [1].
I can't place holds on items. Please place holds through your library account at https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/ or ask a librarian for help.
